design_philosophy.core_loop: Single-player tournament progression through ranked fights, defeating increasingly difficult opponents to earn colored belts and unlock playable bosses
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Learning and executing character-specific move sets against diverse opponent archetypes with escalating difficulty and boss phases
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Commanding absurd, sphere-based fighter archetypes (clown, sumo, ballerina, dinosaur) in a lighthearted martial arts tournament
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Deliberately unconventional fighter roster with comedic personalities (farting monkey, tattooing clown) paired with pseudo-3D sphere-based visual aesthetic; uses humor as primary differentiation rather than serious martial arts simulation. Intentionally provocative marketing (original 'gotta have Ballz' tagline)

difficulty.checkpointing: Tournament bracket structure with individual match progression
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Three selectable difficulty levels spanning tournament progression

progression.upgrade_system: Cosmetic belt unlocks (Red, Green, Blue, Black) that change fighter coloring scheme upon boss defeats; unlocking playable boss characters after defeating final boss
progression.unlock_mechanism: Sequential boss defeats in one-player tournament mode
replayability_hooks: Unlockable boss characters for continued play
Multiple fighter archetypes with distinct move sets and playstyles

world_building_techniques: The Jester character established as tournament organizer in opening cutscene
Character archetypes with minimal contextual lore (sumo wrestler, caveman, ballerina)
Boss progression implies escalating challenge tier

boss_design.philosophy: Escalating opponent specialization: each boss exhibits distinct combat archetype and signature move set; final boss includes multi-phase engagement requiring sustained adaptation
notable_bosses: Guggler (Ostrich): Pecking and beak throws with jump-kick mix-ups
Bounder (Kangaroo): Boxing gloves, jumping patterns, tail-based leg sweeps
T-Wrecks (Dinosaur): Aggressive jaw-grab grapples and ground-shock tail attacks
Lamprey (Genie): Swift magical attacks with animal transformation abilities (bull, scorpion, snake forms)
The Jester (Ultimate Boss): Multi-phase encounter with disassembly mechanic, high damage output, reassembly pattern requiring two defeat cycles to grant boss unlock reward

faction_indicators: Tournament bracket hierarchy (Guggler → Bounder → T-Wrecks → Lamprey → Jester progression suggests tiered power scaling)
danger_signals: The Jester's disassembly/reassembly mechanic indicates final boss requiring sustained pattern learning and health management
T-Wrecks' jaw-grab and ground-shock combo suggests grapple-based damage spikes
environmental_storytelling: Belt color unlocks mark tournament advancement stages
Character archetypes (caveman with club, ballerina with grace moves) imply environmental/cultural flavor despite minimal narrative
